findings.short_text: random_forest on orbital_only is the current defensible model-evidence view.
findings.technical_basis: The evidence builder excludes definition-heavy feature sets from the best defensible pick. Model evidence supports interpretation; it does not define the priority ranking.
findings.title: Best defensible evidence avoids definition-heavy leakage

best_defensible_model.interpretation: This model is useful as secondary evidence because it avoids direct definition-heavy features and focuses on orbital or contextual signals.
limitations: May understate patterns that depend on physical-size or MOID definition fields.
best_defensible_model.not_recommended_use: Do not treat this as an official impact-risk or alerting model.
best_defensible_model.recommended_use: Defensible support for pattern consistency without PHA definition variables.
strengths: Provides a measurable check on positive-label retrieval.

findings.short_text: 310 objects are flagged in disagreement outputs.
findings.technical_basis: Disagreements compare high-confidence predictions against observed labels or model families and remain secondary to the Risk Priority Score.
findings.title: Model disagreements create an inspection queue

findings.short_text: Definition-heavy or score-derived feature sets are retained as diagnostics, not primary evidence.
findings.technical_basis: Leakage-sensitive models are identified from feature-set names and model-card metadata.
findings.title: Some high-performing model views are leakage-sensitive
